$comment: Canonical source: packages/foundry/src/schemas/galaxy-workflow-test-plan/galaxy-workflow-test-plan.schema.json in galaxyproject/foundry. Mold frontmatter cites this schema via [[galaxy-workflow-test-plan]] wiki-links; the cast pipeline imports the `galaxyWorkflowTestPlanSchema` runtime export and serializes it into cast bundles. The on-disk artifact is YAML; validate it with `foundry validate-galaxy-workflow-test-plan`.
title: Galaxy Workflow Test Plan
description: Intermediate, reviewable Galaxy workflow test-plan handoff produced by a *-test-to-galaxy-test-plan Mold (nextflow, cwl, or freeform) and consumed by implement-galaxy-workflow-test. It preserves test intent, fixture provenance, assertion intent, tolerances, label assumptions, unresolved mappings, and intentional omissions before any concrete tests-format `*-tests.yml` is authored. It is NOT the final test artifact: assertion vocabulary is referenced by family name from the tests-format schema, not duplicated here.

test_cases.description: One entry per planned Galaxy workflow test (each becomes one tests-format test entry downstream).

unresolved.description: Mappings the plan could not resolve and that implement-galaxy-workflow-test (or a reviewer) must settle before authoring the final test file.

omissions.description: Outputs or behaviors deliberately left unasserted, with rationale, so the gap is a recorded decision rather than an oversight.

warnings.description: Plan-construction warnings: source-test evidence that could not be translated, expression-derived shapes, missing fixtures, and similar.

kind.description: Source family of the Galaxy-targeting translator that produced this plan.

name.description: Human-oriented workflow or source name the plan was derived from.

derived_from.description: Dominant basis for the plan: test-evidence means it was translated from existing upstream test fixtures/snapshots/job files (nf-test, CWL jobs); intent means it was synthesized from workflow intent and scenario-level expected outputs without upstream test evidence (freeform paper/interview sources).

notes.description: Free-text provenance about the source and how test evidence was (or was not) available.

WorkflowRef.description: How the plan relates to the Galaxy workflow it tests, and where its input/output labels came from.

label_source.description: Provenance of the input/output labels this plan references: draft means they were read from a concrete gxformat2 workflow draft (verifiable); interface-brief means they came from an upstream Galaxy interface brief; assumed means they were inferred and are unconfirmed against any workflow.

notes.description: Free-text notes about label confidence and workflow-binding assumptions.

id.description: Stable identifier for this planned test case.

doc.description: What this test exercises, in plain language; becomes the tests-format test `doc`.

derived_from.description: Basis for this case: translated from upstream test evidence, synthesized from intent, or a mix.

provenance.description: Where this case came from, e.g. an nf-test name, a CWL job file, a paper section, or an interview answer.

job_inputs.description: Planned workflow job inputs for this case.

expected_outputs.description: Planned workflow outputs to assert for this case.

workflow_label.description: Workflow input label this job entry binds to; null when the label is not yet known.

label_status.description: resolved means the label matches a concrete workflow draft input; assumed means it is inferred from a brief; unresolved means the binding label is not yet decided.

description.description: What this input is and the role it plays in the test.

collection_shape.description: Galaxy collection shape when the input is a collection, e.g. list, paired, list:paired, list:list; null for a single dataset or when unknown.

datatype.description: Galaxy datatype/extension hint, e.g. fastqsanger.gz, fasta, tabular; null when undecided.

storage.description: Where the fixture data lives or should live: remote-url (Zenodo/EBI/SRA location), in-repo (committed test-data path), cvmfs-string (data-table/built-in-index bare string), generated-toy (to be synthesized), or unresolved (not yet sourced).

location.description: Concrete URL, repo-relative path, or data-table string when known; null when the fixture still needs resolution.

checksum.description: Integrity hash when available, e.g. SHA-1:<hex>; null when unknown.

provenance.description: Where this fixture comes from or how it was chosen, e.g. a translated source test-data reference, a Zenodo record, or a note that test-data-resolution must supply it.

workflow_label.description: Workflow output label to assert against; null when the output label is not yet known.

label_status.description: resolved means the label matches a concrete workflow draft output; assumed means it is inferred from a brief; unresolved means the output label is not yet decided.

description.description: What this output is and why it is worth asserting.

output_kind.description: Whether the output is a single dataset or a collection; null when undecided. Collection outputs carry element-level assertion intent via assertion_intent[].element_identifier.

collection_shape.description: Galaxy collection shape for collection outputs, e.g. list, list:paired, list:list; null otherwise.

assertion_intent.description: Assertion intent for this output. May be empty when the output is intentionally unasserted (record the reason in omissions). Each entry names a tests-format assertion family by name rather than duplicating its full parameter object.

family.description: tests-format assertion family or compare operator this intent maps to, by name, e.g. has_text, has_n_lines, has_n_columns, has_size, has_image_width, has_h5_keys, file, compare:diff, compare:sim_size. Names reference the tests-format schema vocabulary; this plan does not restate parameter shapes.

intent.description: What this assertion verifies, in plain language, so implement-galaxy-workflow-test can materialize the concrete assertion without re-reading the source tests.

expected_value.description: Concrete expected token, count, size, or comparison fixture when known; null when synthesized from intent without a concrete value.

tolerance.description: Tolerance for the expected value when applicable; null for exact or existence-only assertions.

element_identifier.description: Collection element identifier this assertion targets, for element-level (element_tests) assertions; null for top-level dataset assertions.

evidence.description: Whether this assertion was translated from upstream test evidence or synthesized from intent.

confidence.description: How firmly the expected value and family are believed correct; synthesized assertions are typically medium or low.

kind.description: Tolerance operator: delta (absolute count/bytes, suffixes like 1K/1M allowed as strings), delta_frac (fraction such as 0.1), lines_diff (number of mutable header lines for compare:diff), or none.

magnitude.description: Tolerance magnitude, e.g. 6, "10K", or 0.1; null when kind is none or the magnitude is undecided.

rationale.description: Why this magnitude was chosen, e.g. mutable VCF header line count, percentage of typical file size, or nondeterministic output.

description.description: What could not be resolved and why.

blocking.description: Whether implement-galaxy-workflow-test must resolve this before authoring a runnable test.

suggested_resolution.description: A proposed way to resolve it when one is apparent; null otherwise.

target.description: What is intentionally left unasserted, e.g. a workflow output label or a class of outputs.

reason.description: Why it is omitted, e.g. nondeterministic content, no stable checkpoint, or asserted only weakly elsewhere.

category.description: Coarse omission category; null when none fits.

code.description: Stable warning code, e.g. untranslatable-snapshot, missing-fixture, expression-derived-shape.

path.description: Pointer into the source or plan the warning refers to; null when not applicable.
